issues.description: The new `toolCall` object includes a `metadata` field (`metadata: msg.metadata || {}`), but the `ToolCall` outbound message type (sidecar/src/types.ts:40-45) has no `metadata` property, and the `ToolCallInfo` interface (claudeStore.ts:10-16) has no `metadata` field. `msg.metadata` will always be `undefined`, making `metadata` always `{}`. More critically, pushing an object with a `metadata` property into a `ToolCallInfo[]` array will fail TypeScript strict type checking since `metadata` is not declared on the interface.
issues.suggested_fix: Either add `metadata?: Record<string, unknown>` to both the `ToolCall` type in types.ts and the `ToolCallInfo` interface in claudeStore.ts, or remove the `metadata` field from the tool call object until the sidecar actually sends it.

issues.description: `resultCache` is assigned to the message object inside `produce()`, but the `AssistantMessage` interface (claudeStore.ts:18-27) has no `resultCache` property. This will fail TypeScript compilation in strict mode. Additionally, `resultCache` is never read anywhere in the codebase—no component or function consumes it—making it dead code that adds complexity without value.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `resultCache?: Record<string, { output: unknown; is_error: boolean }>` to the `AssistantMessage` interface if this feature is needed, and add corresponding consumer code. Otherwise, remove the resultCache logic entirely.

issues.description: The `ToolResult` outbound message type (sidecar/src/types.ts:47-52) has fields `tool_name` and `result`, but the diff reads `msg.output` and `msg.is_error` which do not exist on the `ToolResult` interface. Also, `msg.tool_call_id` is referenced but the `ToolResult` type has no `tool_call_id` field—it only has `id` (the assistant message id) and `tool_name`. These property accesses will always return `undefined`, and the resultCache will contain `{ output: undefined, is_error: undefined }` for every entry.
issues.suggested_fix: Use the actual `ToolResult` fields: `msg.result` for the output and `msg.tool_name` for the key. If `tool_call_id`, `output`, and `is_error` are being added to the sidecar protocol, update the `ToolResult` interface in types.ts first.

issues.location: gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts:160-163 (diff: tool_call case, toolCall ID computation)
issues.description: The tool call ID is now computed outside `produce()` by reading `state.messages[idx]` through the reactive proxy with `as any` cast: `(state.messages[idx] as any).toolCalls.length`. The original code computed it inside `produce()` using the mutable draft (`m.toolCalls.length`), which is guaranteed to reflect the latest state. While SolidJS applies store mutations synchronously (so this works in practice), the pattern bypasses type safety and is more fragile than the original. If SolidJS ever changes batching semantics, or if the store is replaced with a different reactivity system, this could produce duplicate IDs.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the ID computation inside the `produce()` callback using the draft object `m.toolCalls.length`, as in the original code. Build the rest of the toolCall object outside if desired, but generate the ID inside produce.

issues.location: gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts:184-190 (diff: tool_result case, resultCache outside produce)
issues.description: The resultCache is constructed by reading state outside `produce()` with `as any` casts, then replacing the entire cache object inside produce. This creates a new plain object each time, causing SolidJS to re-wrap it and trigger reactivity for all cache entries, not just the one that changed. It also means two rapid `tool_result` events could theoretically create a race if SolidJS ever defers mutations.
issues.suggested_fix: If resultCache is needed, perform all mutations inside `produce()` to leverage SolidJS's fine-grained reactivity: `if (!m.resultCache) m.resultCache = {}; m.resultCache[msg.tool_call_id] = { ... };`

opus_bug_hunter.summary: The diff has three blocking issues stemming from mismatches between the code and its type contracts. First, `msg.metadata` is read from the `ToolCall` message but no such field exists on the `ToolCall` interface—it will always be `undefined`. Second, `resultCache` is assigned to `AssistantMessage` objects but isn't declared on the interface, which will fail strict TypeScript compilation and is never consumed by any component (dead code). Third and most critically, the `tool_result` handler reads `msg.output`, `msg.is_error`, and `msg.tool_call_id` from the `ToolResult` message, but the `ToolResult` type only has `id`, `tool_name`, and `result`—all three property accesses will return `undefined`, making the resultCache entries meaningless. Additionally, the pattern of computing values outside `produce()` with `as any` casts is more fragile than the original code which correctly operated on the mutable draft inside `produce()`.

issues.description: The toolCall object is built outside the produce() callback by reading state via `(state.messages[idx] as any).toolCalls.length`. The `as any` cast bypasses the discriminated union, and worse, the toolCalls array length is read *before* the produce mutation runs, creating a snapshot that may be stale if multiple tool_call messages arrive in the same batch. The original code read `m.toolCalls.length` inside produce(), which was always correct.
issues.suggested_fix: Move the toolCall construction back inside the produce() callback, reading `m.toolCalls.length` from the draft message `m` directly, which is both type-safe and always current.

issues.description: The diff adds a `resultCache` property to AssistantMessage via `m.resultCache = resultCache`, but `AssistantMessage` has no `resultCache` field. This is an undeclared property mutation that violates the interface contract. It will work at runtime in JS but breaks type safety and signals the type definition is out of sync with usage. Additionally, building the cache object outside produce() using `(state.messages[idx] as any).resultCache` repeats the same `as any` escape hatch.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `resultCache?: Record<string, { output: unknown; is_error: boolean }>` to the `AssistantMessage` interface, and build/update the cache inside the produce() callback to stay type-safe.

issues.description: The resultCache duplicates information already stored on individual ToolCallInfo objects (which have a `result` field and a `status` field). ToolCallInfo.result already captures the tool output, and status captures success/error. Adding a parallel lookup cache creates two sources of truth for the same data, increasing the risk of desynchronization.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider whether the existing ToolCallInfo.result field is sufficient. If O(1) lookup by tool_call_id is truly needed, derive it lazily (e.g., a memo/getter) rather than storing a mutable copy on the message.

issues.description: Both new code paths cast `state.messages[idx] as any` to access `.toolCalls` and `.resultCache`. This pattern circumvents the discriminated union narrowing that already exists inside the produce() callback (via `if (m.role !== 'assistant') return`). Scattering `as any` outside produce undermines the type system the codebase is built on.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep all message field access inside the produce() callback where the discriminated union is properly narrowed, eliminating the need for `as any`.

issues.description: The `metadata` field (`msg.metadata || {}`) is added to the toolCall object but ToolCallInfo has no `metadata` property. This is an undeclared field that will be silently ignored by type-aware consumers and invisible in IDE autocomplete.
issues.suggested_fix: If metadata is needed, add `metadata?: Record<string, unknown>` to the ToolCallInfo interface. If it's not yet consumed, defer adding it until the consumer exists.

opus_design_critic.summary: The diff moves state reads outside of SolidJS produce() callbacks to pre-build objects, but in doing so introduces `as any` casts that bypass the discriminated union type system the store is built on. This creates two blocking issues: (1) the tool call ID computation reads a potentially stale array length outside the mutation boundary, and (2) a new `resultCache` property is written onto AssistantMessage without updating the interface, violating the type contract. The resultCache also duplicates data already available on ToolCallInfo.result, creating a second source of truth. The metadata field is similarly untyped. All object construction should remain inside the produce() callback where the union is properly narrowed.
